exercises.name: Seated Resistance Band Row
exercises.startingPosition: Sit tall in a stable chair or wheelchair with the band secured safely.
steps: Hold the band with one or both hands as comfortable.
exercises.easierAlternative: Use a lighter band or reduce the range of motion.
exercises.harderProgression: Increase band resistance or add a pause.
exercises.voiceGuidance: Sit tall. Pull the band slowly toward you. Pause, then return with control.
exercises.visualDescription: A seated person pulls a resistance band toward their torso with controlled movement.
exercises.confidenceNote: Controlled pulling builds strength while keeping your body supported.

exercises.name: Seated March
exercises.startingPosition: Sit tall in a stable chair with feet on the floor if comfortable.
steps: Repeat on the other side if comfortable.
safetyNotes: Keep movement small if hips or knees feel uncomfortable.
exercises.harderProgression: Increase time or slow the lowering phase.
exercises.voiceGuidance: Lift one knee gently, lower with control, then switch sides if comfortable.
exercises.visualDescription: A seated person gently lifts one knee at a time like marching in place.
exercises.confidenceNote: Small controlled movements can build confidence and circulation.

exercises.name: Wall Push
exercises.startingPosition: Stand facing a wall with hands on the wall at comfortable height.
steps: Place your hand or hands on the wall.
Bend your elbow slowly toward the wall.
safetyNotes: Use one-arm version if only one arm is available.
exercises.harderProgression: Stand slightly farther away if stable.
exercises.seatedAlternative: Seated forward press with a resistance band if available.
exercises.voiceGuidance: Place your hand on the wall. Bend slowly, then press back with control.
exercises.visualDescription: A person presses gently against a wall with controlled elbow movement.
exercises.confidenceNote: This builds pushing strength without needing the floor.

exercises.name: Seated Core Rotation
exercises.startingPosition: Sit tall with feet supported if possible.
steps: Place hands comfortably on your lap or across your chest.
Rotate your torso slightly to one side.
Rotate gently to the other side if comfortable.
exercises.harderProgression: Hold each side for two seconds.
exercises.voiceGuidance: Sit tall. Rotate gently to one side, return to center, then switch if comfortable.
exercises.visualDescription: A seated person gently rotates their torso from side to side.
exercises.confidenceNote: Gentle rotation can support core awareness and control.

exercises.name: Chair-Supported Calf Raise
exercises.startingPosition: Stand behind a stable chair and hold it lightly for support.
exercises.voiceGuidance: Hold the chair. Rise gently onto your toes, pause, and lower slowly.
exercises.visualDescription: A standing person holds a chair and gently raises their heels.
exercises.confidenceNote: Support makes balance training safer and more controlled.

exercises.name: Seated Heel Raise
steps: Keep toes on the floor if possible.
safetyNotes: Keep the movement comfortable and pain-free.
exercises.harderProgression: Add a longer pause at the top.
exercises.voiceGuidance: Lift your heels gently, pause, and lower with control.
exercises.visualDescription: A seated person raises and lowers their heels while keeping toes supported.
exercises.confidenceNote: This supports lower-leg movement without standing.

exercises.name: Seated Shoulder Circles
steps: Keep the movement small if needed.
safetyNotes: Avoid large circles if shoulder discomfort appears.
exercises.voiceGuidance: Roll your shoulders gently. Keep the movement slow and comfortable.
exercises.visualDescription: A seated person makes small controlled shoulder circles.
exercises.confidenceNote: Gentle mobility can help you start moving with ease.
